text: A 65-year-old male presents with sudden onset of palpitations. ECG shows a narrow complex tachycardia with no visible P waves. Diagnosis?
rationale: AVNRT typically presents as a regular narrow complex tachycardia. The absence of P waves or retrograde P waves is common.

text: A newborn presents with excessive salivation and choking during the first feeding. A nasogastric tube cannot be passed. Diagnosis?
rationale: Choking during the first feed and inability to pass an NG tube are classic signs of Esophageal Atresia.
